heading: Silver star: award
label: 9276 Silver star: award
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)874610:1412.(words after 2d semicolon of 12th par. under “Ordnance Department”); restated (last sentence); restated , .July 9, 1918, ch. 143Jan. 24, 1920, ch. 55, § 1Dec. 15, 1942, ch. 73656 Stat. 1052The words “may award” are inserted to conform to other sections of this chapter. The words “if the person earned” are inserted for clarity. The words “commanded by” are omitted as surplusage.

Amendments2021— inserted “or the Space Force” after “the Air Force” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 116–2832018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232section 8746 of this title1963— substituted provisions permitting the issuance of a silver star for gallantry while engaged in an action against an enemy of the United States, while engaged in military operations involving conflict with an opposing foreign force, or while serving with friendly foreign forces engaged in an armed conflict against an opposing armed force in which the United States is not a belligerent party, and requiring it to be of appropriate design, for provisions which authorized the issuance of the silver star for gallantry in action and which required that the silver star be three-sixteenths of an inch in diameter, the citation thereof be published in orders issued from the headquarters of a force that is the appropriate command of a general officer, and that it be worn as directed by the President.Pub. L. 88–77

Effective Date of 2018 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with provision for the coordination of amendments and special rule for certain redesignations, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 115–232section 800 of Pub. L. 115–232section 3001 of this titleFeb. 1, 2019

children.text: while engaged in an action against an enemy of the United States;
while engaged in military operations involving conflict with an opposing foreign force; or
while serving with friendly foreign forces engaged in an armed conflict against an opposing armed force in which the United States is not a belligerent party.
